judge_scores.reasoning: The response incorrectly states that Romeu Zema can run for re-election as governor in 2026, confidently claiming he has the right after his 2018 and 2022 elections, despite him having exhausted two consecutive terms (2019-2022 and 2023-2026) per Brazilian constitutional limits.
Incorrect and confident: Zema has already served two consecutive terms and is ineligible to run again for governor in 2026.

The response incorrectly states that Zema can run for governor reelection in 2026, when in fact he has already served two consecutive terms (2019-2022 and 2023-2026) and has reached the constitutional limit for state executive reelection, making him ineligible.
